name: Polynesian Pork Centennial
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim excess fat from chops; lightly grease a heavy skillet with fat from one chop. Brown the chops on both sides, then drain off excess fat. Season lightly with salt and pepper.
2. Drain pineapple (from a medium can, using sweetened juice or its own juice as desired). Combine the juice, onion, celery leaves, garlic, prunes, soy sauce, and marjoram in a separate bowl. Pour the mixture over the chops.
3. Simmer, covered, for 20-30 minutes (depending on thickness of chops).
4. Add pineapple and celery to the skillet, then cook for an additional 10 minutes or until the celery is tender and pork is well done. If needed, add a small amount of water.
5. Prepare rice according to package directions; stir in ginger to blend. Serve pork over rice.
